channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: The Latest: Harris keeps a focus on 'blue wall' states in the campaign's final weeks
item.description: With three weeks left in the presidential campaign, Democrat Kamala Harris is spending most of her days trying to shore up support in the “blue wall” states of Pennsylvania, Michigan and Wisconsin as she tries to avoid a repeat of Hillary Clinton’s collapse there eight years ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Texas appeals court reverses judge's order to delay execution in shaken-baby case, last appeal filed
item.description: HUNTSVILLE, Texas (AP) — A Texas appellate court reversed a judge's order to delay Thursday night's planned execution of a man who would become the first person in the U.S. to be put to death for a murder conviction tied to a diagnosis of shaken baby syndrome.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO and MICHAEL GRACZYK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Missing teen kayaker found clinging to kayak off Waikiki
item.description: "I think he was in total shock because he wasn't emotional at all. I was actually crying my guts off because he was okay.
item.dc:creator: Nicole Napuunoa, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Menendez brothers' uncle requests DA meeting; says boys' motive was greed
item.description: Anderson believes Erik and Lyle Menendez weren't molested by their father Jose and that "justice was served.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, Fox 5

item.title: Alabama executes man who killed 5 and asked to be put to death
item.description: ATMORE, Ala. (AP) — Alabama executed a man Thursday who admitted to killing five people with an ax and gun during a drug-fueled rampage in 2016 and dropped his appeals to allow his lethal injection to go forward. Derrick Dearman, 36, was pronounced dead at 6:14 p.m.
item.dc:creator: BY KIM CHANDLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Alyssa Lokits case: Man accused of savagely murdering woman on Nashville trail makes first court appearance
item.description: Family and friends of the Nashville woman who was slain while out exercising on a popular trail are mourning her death as the suspect in connection to her murder is set to make his first court appearance.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: California man tortured girlfriend, tattooed ‘degrading words’ on her chest before killing her
item.description: A California man who allegedly tortured his girlfriend by tattooing her with "degrading words" has been convicted after her Christmas Eve murder.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Daughter claims mother's body 'misplaced' in Memphis cemetery
item.description: "They have no idea where or how she was lost, but they do not know where she is," says Georleshia Minter.
item.dc:creator: Quametra Wilborn, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 3 states renew efforts to limit abortion pill access
item.description: The lawsuit was filed in a Texas federal court, roughly four months after the Supreme Court decided against undoing changes to the FDA’s regulations on the abortion pill that expanded access.
item.dc:creator: Alejandra O'Connell-Domenech, NewsNation

item.title: Indian intelligence official charged in murder-for-hire plot on Sikh separatist leader in New York City
item.description: An Indian government employee is accused of plotting the murder-for-hire of a Sikh separatist leader in New York City, the Justice Department said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israel's foreign minister says Hamas' top leader Yahya Sinwar was killed in Gaza
item.description: Israel’s foreign minister has confirmed that Israeli troops in Gaza have killed Hamas’ top leader Yahya Sinwar, a chief architect of last year's attack on Israel that sparked the war.
item.dc:creator: Wafaa Shurafa, Samy Magdy, Jack Jeffery, KTLA 5

item.title: Gulf War illness may increase risk for heart disease or stroke
item.description: The illness also was associated with more than double the odds of being diagnosed with diabetes and high blood pressure, according to the study, which was published Friday in the Journal of the American Heart Association.
item.dc:creator: Michael Merschel, American Heart Association News, NewsNation

item.title: Florida homeowners fear soaring insurance cost after hurricanes
item.description: With 4 major hurricanes hitting Florida in 4 years, insurance premiums have skyrocketed and insurers have pulled back on coverage; residents are worried about more exclusions and price hikes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Liam Payne died from 'multiple traumas,' 'internal and external bleeding,' autopsy reveals
item.description: Officials in Argentina have released a cause of death for Liam Payne, the former One Direction member who died on Wednesday at the age of 31.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WFLA, NBC

item.title: How you can help protect your aging parents from scammers
item.description: "They were raised to be a very trusting generation, a very rule following generation, so they make the assumption that everyone has their best interest at heart," Paula Daigle said.
item.dc:creator: Hayley Wielgus, NewsNation

item.title: Biggest source of new Floridians and Texans last year was other countries
item.description: ORLANDO, Fla. (AP) — The biggest source of new residents to Florida and Texas, the two U.S. states with the largest number of new residents last year, was other countries.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SCHNEIDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Liam Payne died from 'multiple traumas,' 'internal and external bleeding,' autopsy reveals
item.description: Officials have released the cause of death of Liam Payne, the former member of the British boy band One Direction, in a preliminary report. According to Argentina's National Criminal and Correctional Prosecutor's Office No.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, KTLA 5

item.title: Homeland Security grants temporary status to Lebanese already in the United States
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration on Thursday announced temporary legal status for Lebanese citizens already in the United States, likely pleasing some Arab voters, including in the battleground state of Michigan.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Liam Payne's family releases statement as more details, autopsy results emerge in singer's death
item.description: Payne, 31, died after a fall from the third story of the Casa Sur hotel in Buenos Aires on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, WFLA, NBC

item.title: La Nina could soon arrive. Here's what that means for winter weather
item.description: The National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration’s Climate Prediction Center says there is a 60% chance that a weak La Nina event will develop this autumn and could last until March.
item.dc:creator: ISABELLA O'MALLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: McConnell privately slammed Trump as ‘stupid’ and ‘despicable’ after 2020 election
item.description: McConnell also criticized the former president as a “narcissist” and admitted after the 2020 election that “it’s not just the Democrats who are counting the days” until he left office.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: McConnell privately slammed Trump as ‘stupid’ and ‘despicable’ after 2020 election
item.description: Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) privately slammed former President Donald Trump as “stupid as well as being ill-tempered” and “a despicable human being” after the 2020 election, according to a biography of the senator set to be released later this month.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, Fox 5

item.title: Catholic Archdiocese of L.A. to pay $880 million in childhood sex abuse claims
item.description: A landmark settlement has been reached with the Catholic Archdiocese of Los Angeles to settle more than 1,300 claims of childhood sexual abuse and assault.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Person whose DNA was found on collar of dead puppy tied to fire hydrant has died
item.description: After a pit bull puppy was found dead, tied to a fire hydrant in Richmond's East End in November of 2023, investigators have released new information and closed the case.
item.dc:creator: Katelyn Harlow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Instagram rolls out new features to prevent sextortion scams targeting teens
item.description: Instagram has introduced a series of new safety features to prevent sextortion schemes, marking the platform's latest effort to protect users, especially teenagers, from online harm.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, Fox 5

item.title: Asian American evangelicals' theology is conservative. But that doesn't mean they vote that way
item.description: The Rev. Wayne Lee leads an English-speaking church of second- and third-generation Chinese Americans in the heart of Philadelphia’s Chinatown.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Brightest supermoon rising this week, and it won't be alone
item.description: Get ready to look up for the year’s biggest and brightest supermoon. And it's pairing up with a comet for a rare stargazing two-for-one.
item.dc:creator: Dominique Jack, KTLA 5

item.title: Former Ozzy Osbourne guitarist Jake E. Lee shot multiple times in Las Vegas
item.description: Las Vegas police said in an emailed statement that the victim was shot around 2:40 a.m. Tuesday and was taken to a hospital for treatment.
item.dc:creator: MARIA SHERMAN, RIO YAMAT and ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Bills acquire wide receiver Amari Cooper from Browns, giving QB Josh Allen a much-needed playmaker
item.description: Amari Cooper went from a fading No. 1 wide receiver in Cleveland to a top one with Buffalo. Looking to add a playmaker for quarterback Josh Allen, the Bills acquired Cooper on Tuesday in a trade with the skidding Browns, whose season seems to be unraveling by the week with the NFL’s worst offense.
item.dc:creator: JOHN WAWROW and TOM WITHERS, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Coca-Cola recalls 'zero sugar' lemonade after it's found to contain full sugar
item.description: The recall, which Coca-Cola issued in September but didn’t announce publicly, applied to 13,152 cases, each with 12 cartons of 12 cans of 12 ounces of lemonade.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: UCLA-led team finds 2 million convicted felons have the right to vote, but they might not know it
item.description: A study led by researchers at UCLA has found that due to voting eligibility expansions, more than 2 million people with felony convictions are now able to vote.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: Survivors and relatives in Maine's deadliest shooting start the process of suing the Army
item.description: LEWISTON, Maine (AP) — Lawyers for 100 survivors and relatives of victims of the deadliest shooting in Maine history have begun the formal process of suing the U.S. Army for what they say was a failure to act to stop the reservist responsible for the crime, attorneys announced Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Walgreens to close 1,200 US stores
item.description: The plan announced Tuesday includes the closing of 300 stores that had been approved under a previous cost-cutting plan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Nearly 200 miles of Blue Ridge Parkway reopens as long-term work remains in North Carolina
item.description: The Blue Ridge Parkway was closed indefinitely after Hurricane Helene swept through the region last month.
item.dc:creator: QCN Digital Staff, WFLA, NBC
